original: Qasim Omar (206) &Javed Miandad(203*) make 397 stand v SL

Findings published of a massive collision of Earth with a large meteorite (S2), 4x the size of Mt. Everest, 3.26 billion years ago, so big it boiled the oceans, but may have kickstarted early evolution
The Parliament of Canada confers Honorary Canadian citizenship on women's rights and education activistMalala Yousafzai
Yulimar Rojas, Venezuelan athlete (Olympic gold triple jump 2020 WR 15.67m), born in Caracas, Venezuela
US 4 x 100m men's relay team of Paul Drayton, Gerry Ashworth, Richard Stebbins & Bob Hayes run world record 39.0s to beat Poland by 0.3s and win the gold medal at the Tokyo Olympics
Phillies win their first World Championship in their 98-year history
Nasa Osiris-Rex spacecraft lands briefly on asteroid Bennu on mission to collect dust, largest sample from space since Apollo
Abebe Bikilaof Ethiopia runs a world record 2:12:11.2 to beat Briton Basil Heatley by more than 4 minutes and win the men's marathon at the Tokyo Olympics; first athlete to win Olympic marathon twice
Finnish Ferrari driverKimi Räikkönenwins season ending Brazilian F1 Grand Prix to claim his first World Drivers Championship by 1 point fromLewis Hamilton
American men's 4×400m relay team beats Great Britain by 0.9s to set world record 3:00.7 and win the gold medal at the Tokyo Olympics; Ollan Cassell, Mike Larrabee, Ulis Williams & Henry Carr
Australian civilian pilot Frederick Valentich vanishes in a Cessna 182 over the Bass Strait south of Melbourne, after reporting contact with an unidentified aircraft.

Ursula K. Le Guin, American science fiction and fantasy author, first woman to win a Hugo Award for Best Novel (Earthsea, The Left Hand of Darkness, Tombs of Atuan), born in Berkeley, California (d. 2018)
Janet Ahlberg, British children's book illustrator with husband Allan (The Jolly Postman, Each Peach Pear Plum, Funnybones), born in Yorkshire, England (d. 1994)
Lavinia Miloşovici, Romanian gymnast (Olympic gold Vault, Floor 1992; 5 x World C'ship gold), born in Lugoj, Romania
Ajaz Patel, New Zealand cricket spin bowler (10-119 v India 2021; one of 3 to take 10 wickets in Test innings), born in Mumbai, India
Buck Helm found alive after being buried 4 days, in San Francisco earthquake
World's oldest natural pearl, 8,000 years old, announced discovered during excavations at Marawah Island, near Abu Dhabi, UAE
The USS Constitution (Old Ironsides) is launched in Boston
Comet Ikeya-Seki approaches perihelion, passing 450,000 kilometers from the sun.
The "grate cheese robbery': scam reported to police of the theft of  rare English Cheddar worth about 300,000 pounds from Neal’s Yard Dairy in London
ExplorerFerdinand Magellanand his fleet reach Cape Virgenes after transiting the Strait ofMagellanbetween the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans, becoming the first Europeans to sail into the Pacific
Austrian Ferrari driverNiki Laudabecomes a 3-time Formula 1 World Drivers champion when he finishes 2nd in the season ending Portuguese Grand Prix at Estoril; wins title by just 0.5 fromAlain Prost
John Birks "Dizzy" Gillespie, American jazz trumpeter, a creator of bebop & modern jazz (A Night In Tunisia), born in Cheraw, South Carolina (d. 1993)
Celia Cruz, Cuban-American Grammy Award-winning guaracha, salsa, and bolero singer (La Sonora Matancera, 1950-65), known as 'the Queen of Salsa', born in Havana, Cuba (d. 2003)
Andre Geim, Russian-British physicist (2010 Nobel Prize for isolation of Graphene), born in Sochi, Russia[1]
Hari Nef, American actress (Transparent) and model (1st openly transgender model on a British cover), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
NHL New York Islanders and goaltender Billy Smith's 1st shut-out win, 5-0 vs Washington Capitals
Fred Dryer of the then Los Angeles Rams becomes the first player in NFL history to score two safeties in the same game.
Images of the dwarf planet Eris are taken and subsequently used in its discovery by the team of Michael E. Brown, Chad Trujillo, and David L. Rabinowitz.
Ayrton Sennaof Brazil crashes his McLaren during the Japanese Grand Prix at Suzuka but clinches his second Formula 1 World Drivers Championship
English scientistJohn Daltonreads his paper "On the Absorption of Gases by Water and Other Liquids" to the Manchester Literary and Philosophical Soc - 1st outline of his atomic theory
Jean-Pierre Sauvage, French chemist, 2016 Nobel Prize for Chemistry for the development of molecular machines, born in Paris, France
Wolfgang Ketterle, German physicist, 2001 Nobel Prize for work on Bose–Einstein condensation, born in Heidelberg, Germany
First display of the word "Liberty" on a flag, raised by colonists in Taunton, Massachusetts, in defiance of British rule in Colonial America
US hostage Jesse Turner released from 5 years in captivity in Beirut
Guggenheim Museum, designed byFrank Lloyd Wright, opens in New York City
Kateri Tekakwitha canonized as the 1st Native American saint by PopeBenedict XVI
Shammi Kapoor, Indian actor and director known as the "Elvis Presley of India" (Amaran, Brahmachari, Vidhaata), born in Bombay, British India (d. 2011)
Osamu Watanabe, Japanese freestyle wrestler (Olympic gold 63kg 1964; World C'ship gold 1962, 63), born in Wassamu, Hokkaido, Japan (d. 2022)
Moses Mayekiso, South African union-leader and anti-apartheid activist, born in Askeaton, Eastern Cape, South Africa
Alexander Malofeev, Russian pianist (Best Young Musician 2017, first Young Yamaha Artist 2017), born in Moscow, Russia[1][2]
Florence Nightingale, with a staff of 38 nurses, is sent to the Crimean War
Jacques Offenbach's operetta "Orpheus in the Underworld" (Orphée aux Enfers) premieres in Paris, includes "Infernal Galop" (can-can tune)
Severe earthquake at 7:53 AM, centered in Hayward, California
Women in France allowed to vote for 1st time
Nobel prize for chemistry awarded to Robert B Woodward
Vivian Beaumont Theater (NYC) opens
Nobel prize of peace awarded to Norman E Borlaugh
Coast Guard Academy 1st allows women to enroll
Author of "Brave New World"Aldous Huxleywrites to congratulateGeorge Orwellon his new novel "Nineteen Eighty-Four"
Film version of "My Fair Lady" directed byGeorge Cukorand starringRex HarrisonandAudrey Hepburn, premieres in New York (Academy Awards Best Picture 1965)

"United We Stand" benefit concert for September 11, 2001 terrorist attacks victims, held at RFK Stadium Washington, D.C. organized and headlined byMichael Jacksonand featuring Aerosmith,Mariah Careyand The Backstreet Boys
Edna Purviance, American silent film actress, worked with Charlie Chaplin (The Kid), born in Paradise Valley, Nevada (d. 1958)
Eduard Pütsep, Estonian freestyle wrestler (Olympic gold 58kg 1924), born in Vastseliina Parish, Estonia (d. 1960)
Martin Gardner, American mathematician and scientific writer (Scientific American math & puzzles columnist), born in Hendersonville, North Carolina (d. 2010)
Manfred Mann [Michael Lubowitz], British rock keyboardist ("Mighty Quinn"; "Blinded By The Light"), born in Johannesburg, Transvaal, Union of South Africa
Steve Cropper, American rock and soul guitarist (Booker T & MGs; The Blues Brothers), songwriter ("Sittin' On) The Dock Of The Bay"), and producer (Otis Redding), born in Willow Springs, Missouri
Elvin Bishop, American blues-rock guitarist and songwriter ("Fooled Around and Fell in Love"), born in Glendale, California,
Charlotte Caffey, American rock guitarist, keyboardist, and vocalist (The Go-Go's - "We Got The Beat"), born in Santa Monica, California
Rich Mullins, American contemporary Christian music singer-songwriter ("Awesome God"), born in Richmond, Indiana (d. 1997)
